purpose: Reference for the Table of Contents (TOC) -- a semantic index at {project_root}/.synapse/toc.md. Covers why the TOC exists, structure (Project Overview + per-directory File Index), entry format (filename, summary, tags, content hash, related, exports), generation via !toc_generate (3-phase: discovery, parallel scan, streaming assembly), incremental update via !toc_update (diff detection with hashes), search via !toc with text/tag/combined modes, content hash usage, monorepo organization, and best practices.
gotchas: Master never reads source files directly -- agents do all file reading during !toc_generate
!toc verifies candidates by reading actual files (prevents stale-entry false positives)
Content hash = first 8 chars of SHA-256
Backward-compatible: legacy TOCs without hash comments treated as unchanged
patterns: Three-phase generation: discovery -> parallel scan -> streaming assembly
Streaming assembly protects against context loss from interruption
Tag-based search: # prefix, multiple tags use AND logic
Result ranking: exact name > summary > tag > related file
One-hop related-file traversal in search results
conventions: Entry format: bold filename, em-dash summary, [tags: ...], <!-- hash:xxxxxxxx -->
Sub-lines for Related and Exports indented
Tag categories: technology, domain, role, feature
Directories with 3+ files get own agent, small directories grouped
